top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Étape Romania's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
L'Étape Romania's rolling hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 50 miles.
When someone attacks on rolling hills, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
50 miles on rolling hills burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
L'Étape Romania's rolling hills rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Étape Romania, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on rolling hills. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Étape Romania. Train it until it feels boring.
At L'Étape Romania, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what L'Étape Romania's rolling hills actually does to you.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Smooth pedaling at 50 miles saves muscle. Bad technique at mile 10 is invisible. At mile 80 it's a cramp.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for L'Étape Romania’s 50 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at L'Étape Romania requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling hills to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and VO2max to prepare you for 50 miles of rolling hills in Bucharest, Romania.
